    55â†’== Overview
    56â†’
    57â†’The Vox Liturgical Specification defines the universal naming system for artifacts across all specifications.
    58â†’A
    59â†’{voslc_cipher}
    60â†’seeds the namespace;
    61â†’{vosls_signet_s}
    62â†’build on it;
    63â†’{vosle_epithet_s}
    64â†’provide human-readable descriptions;
    65â†’{vosli_inscription_s}
    66â†’are complete names;
    67â†’{voslv_vesture_s}
    68â†’define domain-specific construction rules.
    69â†’
    70â†’This vocabulary applies to ALL specifications â€” VOS (kit distribution), BUS (bash dispatch), JJD (gallops data), and others.
    71â†’The liturgy is the shared language for naming things.
    72â†’
    73â†’For tabtarget dispatch vocabulary (Colophon, Frontispiece, Imprint, Formulary, Launcher), see BUS-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc.
    74â†’
    75â†’== Core Liturgy Terms
    76â†’
    77â†’[[voslc_cipher]]
    78â†’// âź¦axl_voices axt_stringâź§
    79â†’{voslc_cipher}::
    80â†’A 2-5 character project root that seeds the namespace.
    81â†’The
    82â†’{voslc_cipher}
    83â†’is globally unique across all projects and serves as the foundation for all derived
    84â†’{vosls_signet_s}.
    85â†’Lowercase letters only; no
    86â†’{voslc_cipher}
    87â†’may be a prefix of another (terminal exclusivity).
    88â†’+
    89â†’Single source of truth: `Tools/vok/vof/src/vofc_registry.rs`
    90â†’+
    91â†’See <<cipher_registry>> for declaration pattern and the complete list.
    92â†’
    93â†’[[vosls_signet]]
    94â†’// âź¦axl_voices axt_stringâź§
    95â†’{vosls_signet}::
    96â†’The complete prefix before the separator in an
    97â†’{vosli_inscription}.
    98â†’A
    99â†’{vosls_signet}
   100â†’is derived from a
   101â†’{voslc_cipher}
   102â†’by extending with additional characters that respect terminal exclusivity.
   103â†’The
   104â†’{vosls_signet}
   105â†’is the identity stamp that binds an artifact to its owning project.
   106â†’+
   107â†’Examples: `jjk` (kit), `jjc` (command prefix), `voslc` (this term's signet).
   108â†’+
   109â†’A
   110â†’{vosls_signet}
   111â†’is extended from its
   112â†’{voslc_cipher}
   113â†’using either an
   114â†’{vosln_ensign}
   115â†’(classification marker) or a
   116â†’{voslm_monogram}
   117â†’(epithet-initial binding).
   118â†’
   119â†’[[vosln_ensign]]
   120â†’// âź¦axl_voices axt_stringâź§
   121â†’{vosln_ensign}::
   122â†’A
   123â†’{vosls_signet}
   124â†’extension that classifies artifact category.
   125â†’The terminal character(s) mark a type or role, independent of the
   126â†’{vosle_epithet}.
   127â†’+
   128â†’Examples:
   129â†’+
   130â†’* `axt_string` â€” `t` = type classification, `string` = epithet
   131â†’* `burc_regime` â€” `rc` = regime-config classification, `regime` = epithet
   132â†’* `axtu_path` â€” `tu` = type-universal sub-classification, `path` = epithet
   133â†’+
   134â†’{vosln_ensign_s}
   135â†’enable hierarchical categorization within a
   136â†’{voslc_cipher}
   137â†’namespace.
   138â†’
   139â†’[[voslm_monogram]]
   140â†’// âź¦axl_voices axt_stringâź§
   141â†’{voslm_monogram}::
   142â†’A
   143â†’{vosls_signet}
   144â†’extension where the terminal character binds to the
   145â†’{vosle_epithet}
   146â†’initial.
   147â†’Creates a mnemonic connection between
   148â†’{vosls_signet}
   149â†’and
   150â†’{vosle_epithet}.
   151â†’+
   152â†’Examples:
   153â†’+
   154â†’* `buc_command` â€” `c` = first letter of `command`
   155â†’* `jjw_workbench` â€” `w` = first letter of `workbench`
   156â†’* `voslc_cipher` â€” `c` = first letter of `cipher`
   157â†’+
   158â†’{voslm_monogram_s}
   159â†’create tight signet-epithet binding but limit each terminal letter to one artifact.
   160â†’
   161â†’[[vosle_epithet]]
   162â†’// âź¦axl_voices axt_stringâź§
   163â†’{vosle_epithet}::
   164â†’The descriptive word after the separator in an
   165â†’{vosli_inscription}.
   166â†’Provides human-readable meaning while the
   167â†’{vosls_signet}
   168â†’provides machine-parseable identity.
   169â†’+
   170â†’Examples: `cipher` in `voslc_cipher`, `heat` in `jjc-heat-mount`.
   171â†’
   172â†’[[vosli_inscription]]
   173â†’// âź¦axl_voices axt_stringâź§
   174â†’{vosli_inscription}::
   175â†’The full artifact name combining
   176â†’{vosls_signet},
   177â†’separator, and
   178â†’{vosle_epithet}.
   179â†’An
   180â†’{vosli_inscription}
   181â†’is the complete, unambiguous identifier for any named artifact.
   182â†’+
   183â†’Structure: `{signet}{separator}{epithet}`
   184â†’+
   185â†’Examples: `voslc_cipher` (underscore separator), `jjc-heat-mount` (hyphen separator).
   186â†’Domain
   187â†’{voslv_vesture_s}
   188â†’may specify additional completion rules (see individual domain definitions).
   189â†’
   190â†’[[voslv_vesture]]
   191â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_entityâź§
   192â†’{voslv_vesture}::
   193â†’Domain-specific construction rules for
   194â†’{vosli_inscription_s}.
   195â†’Each
   196â†’{voslv_vesture}
   197â†’defines signet_case, separator, epithet_case, and envelope for its domain.
   198â†’Different domains (Rust, Bash, AsciiDoc, etc.) have different naming conventions.
   199â†’+
   200â†’A
   201â†’{voslv_vesture}
   202â†’is like clothing for a domain â€” it dresses the abstract naming concepts in domain-appropriate form.
   203â†’
   204â†’== Liturgy Domains
   205â†’
   206â†’[[vosldr_rust]]
   207â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_entityâź§
   208â†’{vosldr_rust}::
   209â†’{voslv_vesture}
   210â†’for Rust source identifiers.
   211â†’+
   212â†’* signet_case: snake_case
   213â†’* separator: `_`
   214â†’* epithet_case: PascalCase for types, snake_case for functions
   215â†’* envelope: none
   216â†’
   217â†’[[vosldb_bash]]
   218â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_entityâź§
   219â†’{vosldb_bash}::
   220â†’{voslv_vesture}
   221â†’for Bash source identifiers.
   222â†’+
   223â†’* signet_case: lowercase
   224â†’* separator: `_`
   225â†’* epithet_case: snake_case
   226â†’* envelope: `.sh` for files
   227â†’
   228â†’[[voslda_asciidoc]]
   229â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_entityâź§
   230â†’{voslda_asciidoc}::
   231â†’{voslv_vesture}
   232â†’for AsciiDoc attribute identifiers.
   233â†’+
   234â†’* signet_case: lowercase
   235â†’* separator: `_`
   236â†’* epithet_case: snake_case
   237â†’* envelope: none (attributes), `[[` `]]` for anchors
   238â†’
   239â†’[[vosldp_publication]]
   240â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_entityâź§
   241â†’{vosldp_publication}::
   242â†’{voslv_vesture}
   243â†’for publication and specification files.
   244â†’+
   245â†’* signet_case: UPPERCASE
   246â†’* separator: `-`
   247â†’* epithet_case: PascalCase
   248â†’* envelope: `.adoc`, `.md`
   249â†’
   250â†’[[vosldg_git]]
   251â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_entityâź§
   252â†’{vosldg_git}::
   253â†’{voslv_vesture}
   254â†’for git refs.
   255â†’+
   256â†’* signet_case: lowercase
   257â†’* separator: `/`
   258â†’* epithet_case: kebab-case
   259â†’* envelope: none
   260â†’
   261â†’[[voslds_slash]]
   262â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_entityâź§
   263â†’{voslds_slash}::
   264â†’{voslv_vesture}
   265â†’for slash command identifiers.
   266â†’+
   267â†’* signet_case: lowercase
   268â†’* separator: `-`
   269â†’* epithet_case: kebab-case
   270â†’* envelope: `.md` for files
   271â†’
   272â†’== Terminal Exclusivity
   273â†’
   274â†’A fundamental constraint on
   275â†’{voslc_cipher_s}
   276â†’and
   277â†’{vosls_signet_s}:
   278â†’a prefix either IS a name or HAS children, never both.
   279â†’
   280â†’Examples:
   281â†’
   282â†’* `rbg` has children (`rbga`, `rbgb`...) â†’ `rbg` cannot name a thing
   283â†’* `rbi` names Image module â†’ `rbia`, `rbib` forbidden
   284â†’
   285â†’This rule ensures unambiguous prefix matching for operations like uninstall (which removes all files matching a
   286â†’{vosls_signet}).
   287â†’
   288â†’Validated at runtime by `vofc_validate_terminal_exclusivity()` in `vofc_registry.rs`.
   289â†’
   290â†’== Minting Workflow
   291â†’
   292â†’Before minting new prefixes:
   293â†’
   294â†’1. **Enumerate namespaces** â€” list every place this name will appear (code, refs, commands, env vars, target paths...)
   295â†’2. **Check reserved suffixes** â€” ensure the suffix matches intended type within the kit
   296â†’3. **Verify terminal exclusivity** â€” search existing trees, ensure no conflicts
   297â†’4. **Document the allocation** â€” add to prefix map in relevant spec or CLAUDE.md
   298â†’
   299â†’[[cipher_registry]]
   300â†’== Cipher Registry
   301â†’
   302â†’{voslc_cipher_s}
   303â†’are declared as const values in `Tools/vok/vof/src/vofc_registry.rs`.
   304â†’The VOF crate (Vox Obscura Foundation) is the single source of truth for all project prefixes across the entire VO/VV ecosystem.
   305â†’
   306â†’=== Declaration Pattern
   307â†’
   308â†’[source,rust]
   309â†’----
   310â†’#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
   311â†’pub struct vofc_Cipher {
   312â†’    prefix: &'static str,
   313â†’    project: &'static str,
   314â†’}
   315â†’
   316â†’impl vofc_Cipher {
   317â†’    pub const fn new(prefix: &'static str, project: &'static str) -> Self {
   318â†’        Self { prefix, project }
   319â†’    }
   320â†’}
   321â†’
   322â†’// Example declarations
   323â†’pub const JJ: vofc_Cipher = vofc_Cipher::new("jj", "Job Jockey");
   324â†’pub const BU: vofc_Cipher = vofc_Cipher::new("bu", "Bash Utilities");
   325â†’----
   326â†’
   327â†’=== Uniqueness Guarantees
   328â†’
   329â†’* **Compile-time**: duplicate const names are compilation errors
   330â†’* **Runtime**: `vofc_validate_terminal_exclusivity()` verifies no prefix is a prefix of another
   331â†’* **Convention**: new ciphers require coordinated addition to `vofc_registry.rs`
   332â†’
   333â†’=== Complete Registry
   334â†’
   335â†’See `Tools/vok/vof/src/vofc_registry.rs` for the authoritative list of all
   336â†’{voslc_cipher_s}.
   337â†’The registry is organized by domain:
   338â†’
   339â†’* Voce Viva Ecosystem (VV, VO)
   340â†’* Portable Kits (JJ, BU, CM, HM)
   341â†’* Recipe Bottle Domain (RB, CRG)
   342â†’* Tools and Utilities (GAD, CCC, LMCI, VSL)
   343â†’* Concept Model Vocabulary (MCM, AXL)
   344â†’* Other Projects (PB, WRS, SRF)
   345â†’
   346â†’=== Utility Functions
   347â†’
   348â†’[source,rust]
   349â†’----
   350â†’// Find cipher by prefix
   351â†’pub fn vofc_find_by_prefix(prefix: &str) -> Option<&'static vofc_Cipher>;
   352â†’
   353â†’// Check if prefix is registered
   354â†’pub fn vofc_is_registered(prefix: &str) -> bool;
   355â†’
   356â†’// Validate terminal exclusivity
   357â†’pub fn vofc_validate_terminal_exclusivity() -> Option<(&'static vofc_Cipher, &'static vofc_Cipher)>;
   358â†’----
   359â†’

   170â†’== Overview
   171â†’
   172â†’The Vox Obscura Specification defines the release and installation infrastructure for Claude Code
   173â†’{vose_kit_s}.
   174â†’{vose_kit_s}
   175â†’are developed in a
   176â†’{vosp_kit_forge}
   177â†’and distributed via
   178â†’{vose_parcel_s}
   179â†’to
   180â†’{vosp_target_repo}
   181â†’consumers.
   182â†’
   183â†’Each
   184â†’{vose_kit}
   185â†’declares itself to the
   186â†’{vose_conclave}
   187â†’through a
   188â†’{vose_whisper}
   189â†’- a builder-pattern API that registers the
   190â†’{vose_kit_p}
   191â†’identity,
   192â†’{vose_managed_section_s},
   193â†’and
   194â†’{vosa_concept_model_s}.
   195â†’
   196â†’The
   197â†’{vosor_release}
   198â†’operation creates a
   199â†’{vose_parcel}
   200â†’containing all
   201â†’{vose_kit}
   202â†’assets.
   203â†’The
   204â†’{vosoi_install}
   205â†’operation deploys a
   206â†’{vose_parcel}
   207â†’to a
   208â†’{vosp_target_repo}.
   209â†’The
   210â†’{vosou_uninstall}
   211â†’operation removes Claude Code integration (commands, hooks, CLAUDE.md sections, vvx binary) while preserving
   212â†’{vose_kit}
   213â†’directories.
   214â†’Uses
   215â†’{vosls_signet}
   216â†’-based prefix matching for command/hook removal.
   217â†’
   218â†’**Related Specifications:**
   219â†’
   220â†’* **VLS-VoxLiturgicalSpec.adoc** â€” Universal naming vocabulary (Cipher, Signet, Epithet, Inscription, Vesture)
   221â†’* **BUS-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc** â€” Tabtarget dispatch vocabulary (Colophon, Frontispiece, Imprint, Formulary, Launcher)
   222â†’
   223â†’== Key Premises
   224â†’
   225â†’[[vosk_prefix_validation]]
   226â†’// âź¦axl_voices axk_premiseâź§
   227â†’{vosk_prefix_validation}::
   228â†’All
   229â†’{vosa_command_s},
   230â†’{vosa_hook_s},
   231â†’{vosa_skill_s},
   232â†’and
   233â†’{vosa_subagent_s}
   234â†’must have
   235â†’{vosls_signet_s}
   236â†’derived from their
   237â†’{vose_kit_p}
   238â†’{voslc_cipher}.
   239â†’{vosor_release}
   240â†’fails if any asset's
   241â†’{vosli_inscription}
   242â†’violates the
   243â†’{voslc_cipher}
   244â†’derivation rules.
   245â†’This constraint enables deterministic
   246â†’{vosou_uninstall}
   247â†’by
   248â†’{vosls_signet}
   249â†’matching.
   250â†’+
   251â†’Validation applies to artifact *file names* only.
   252â†’Internal content (function names, variable names within scripts) is not validated during
   253â†’{vosor_release}.
   254â†’
   255â†’[[vosk_all_or_none]]
   256â†’// âź¦axl_voices axk_premiseâź§
   257â†’{vosk_all_or_none}::
   258â†’{vosoi_install}
   259â†’is atomic.
   260â†’A
   261â†’{vose_parcel}
   262â†’represents a tested, coherent release.
   263â†’No selective installation of individual
   264â†’{vose_kit_s}
   265â†’or assets.
   266â†’
   267â†’[[vosk_parcel_self_contained]]
   268â†’// âź¦axl_voices axk_premiseâź§
   269â†’{vosk_parcel_self_contained}::
   270â†’A
   271â†’{vose_parcel}
   272â†’contains everything needed for installation.
   273â†’No dependency resolution or external fetches during
   274â†’{vosoi_install}.
   275â†’The
   276â†’{vose_parcel}
   277â†’can function from its extracted directory.
   278â†’
   279â†’[[vosk_burc_prerequisite]]
   280â†’// âź¦axl_voices axk_premiseâź§
   281â†’{vosk_burc_prerequisite}::
   282â†’{vosoi_install}
   283â†’requires BURC (BUK Regime Configuration) in the
   284â†’{vosp_target_repo}.
   285â†’The install script reads `.buk/burc.env` for required variables:
   286â†’`BURC_PROJECT_ROOT` (path from burc.env's location to project root),
   287â†’`BURC_TOOLS_DIR` (relative to project root), and
   288â†’`BURC_MANAGED_KITS` (comma-separated list of kits included in vvx).
   289â†’Install fails immediately if any required variable is missing â€” no bootstrap, no prompts.
   290â†’
   291â†’[[vosk_default_routing]]
   292â†’// âź¦axl_voices axk_premiseâź§
   293â†’{vosk_default_routing}::
   294â†’Assets without special routing rules copy in place, preserving directory structure relative to
   295â†’{vose_kit}
   296â†’root.
   297â†’Only
   298â†’{vosa_command_s}
   299â†’and
   300â†’{vosa_hook_s}
   301â†’have special routing (to `.claude/commands/` and `.claude/hooks/` respectively).
   302â†’All other files install to `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/{kit}/` maintaining their source-relative paths.
   303â†’

   367â†’== Types
   368â†’
   369â†’[[vost_hallmark]]
   370â†’// âź¦axl_voices axt_stringâź§
   371â†’{vost_hallmark}::
   372â†’A 4-digit version identifier starting at 1000.
   373â†’{vost_hallmark_s}
   374â†’increment for each unique content set during
   375â†’{vosor_release}.
   376â†’Unlike
   377â†’{vost_sigil}
   378â†’(timestamp-based),
   379â†’{vost_hallmark}
   380â†’represents content identity â€” same content always produces same
   381â†’{vost_hallmark}.
   382â†’+
   383â†’Examples: `1000`, `1001`, `1042`.
   384â†’
   385â†’[[vost_sigil]]
   386â†’// âź¦axl_voices axt_stringâź§
   387â†’{vost_sigil}::
   388â†’A version identifier in YYMMDD-HHMM format.
   389â†’Generated at
   390â†’{vosor_release}
   391â†’time.
   392â†’Appears in
   393â†’{vose_parcel}
   394â†’filenames and manifests.
   395â†’+
   396â†’Example: `260115-1430` for January 15, 2026 at 14:30.
   397â†’
   398â†’== Entities
   399â†’
   400â†’[[vose_parcel]]
   401â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_entityâź§
   402â†’{vose_parcel}::
   403â†’The distribution archive for
   404â†’{vose_kit_s}.
   405â†’Named `vvk-parcel-{hallmark}.tar.gz`.
   406â†’Contains platform binaries and plain-text
   407â†’{vose_kit}
   408â†’assets.
   409â†’+
   410â†’Structure:
   411â†’+
   412â†’----
   413â†’vvk-parcel-1000/
   414â†’  vvi_install.sh          # Install script (BCG-style, not installed)
   415â†’  vvbf_brand.json         # Brand file (runtime identity)
   416â†’  kits/
   417â†’    buk/
   418â†’      buc_command.sh
   419â†’      ...
   420â†’    jjk/
   421â†’      jju_utility.sh
   422â†’      ...
   423â†’    vvk/
   424â†’      bin/
   425â†’        vvx-darwin-arm64
   426â†’        vvx-darwin-x86_64
   427â†’        vvx-linux-x86_64
   428â†’      vvu_uninstall.sh
   429â†’  claude/
   430â†’    commands/
   431â†’      jjc-heat-mount.md
   432â†’      cma-normalize.md
   433â†’      vvc-commit.md
   434â†’    hooks/                # Future
   435â†’----
   436â†’+
   437â†’The `kits/` directory mirrors the target `Tools/` structure: `kits/*` becomes `Tools/*`.
   438â†’The `claude/` directory contains Claude Code configuration assets collected from kit forges and routed to `.claude/*` in the target.
   439â†’VVK is a
   440â†’{vose_kit}
   441â†’like any other â€” it just happens to contain binaries.
   442â†’+
   443â†’**Scripts:**
   444â†’+
   445â†’* `vvi_install.sh` â€” Bootstrap script at archive root. Takes `burc.env` path as argument; Rust validates file and parses needed variables (`BURC_TOOLS_DIR`, `BURC_PROJECT_ROOT`). Not installed to target.
   446â†’* `vvu_uninstall.sh` â€” Lives in `kits/vvk/`, installs to `${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/vvk/`.
   447â†’
   448â†’[[vose_whisper]]
   449â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_entityâź§
   450â†’{vose_whisper}::
   451â†’A
   452â†’{vose_kit_p}
   453â†’declaration to the
   454â†’{vose_conclave}.
   455â†’Implemented as a Rust builder API in the
   456â†’{vose_kit_p}
   457â†’{vosp_veiled}
   458â†’directory.
   459â†’+
   460â†’Builder API:
   461â†’+
   462â†’[source,rust]
   463â†’----
   464â†’use voci_ciphers::JJ;
   465â†’
   466â†’Whisper::for_kit("jjk")
   467â†’    .cipher(JJ)                 // symbolic reference to const
   468â†’    .display_name("Job Jockey")
   469â†’    .managed_section("vocjjmc_core.md", "JJK")
   470â†’    .managed_section("vocjjmc_gallops.md", "JJK_GALLOPS")
   471â†’    .concept_model("JJD-GallopsData.adoc")
   472â†’    .register(&mut conclave)
   473â†’----
   474â†’+
   475â†’File location: `Tools/{kit}/vov_veiled/vo{cipher}w_whisper.rs`
   476â†’
   477â†’[[vose_conclave]]
   478â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_entityâź§
   479â†’{vose_conclave}::
   480â†’The central API that
   481â†’{vose_kit_s}
   482â†’use to describe themselves.
   483â†’Lives in VOK as `vocv_conclave.rs`.
   484â†’Collects
   485â†’{vose_whisper_s}
   486â†’and validates
   487â†’{vosk_prefix_validation}
   488â†’during
   489â†’{vosor_release}.
   490â†’+
   491â†’Responsibilities:
   492â†’+
   493â†’* Collect
   494â†’{vose_whisper_s}
   495â†’from all participating
   496â†’{vose_kit_s}
   497â†’* Enforce
   498â†’{vosk_prefix_validation}
   499â†’during
   500â†’{vosor_release}
   501â†’* Provide asset enumeration for
   502â†’{vosoi_install}
   503â†’and
   504â†’{vosou_uninstall}
   505â†’
   506â†’[[vose_kit]]
   507â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_entityâź§
   508â†’{vose_kit}::
   509â†’A distributable Claude Code extension.
   510â†’Lives in `Tools/{kit}/` in the
   511â†’{vosp_kit_forge}.
   512â†’+
   513â†’Structure:
   514â†’+
   515â†’----
   516â†’Tools/{kit}/
   517â†’  {kit}a_arcanum.sh      # Public - install script
   518â†’  {kit}u_utility.sh      # Public - utilities
   519â†’  README.md              # Public - documentation
   520â†’  commands/              # Public - slash commands
   521â†’    {cipher}c-*.md
   522â†’  vov_veiled/            # Private - never distributed
   523â†’    vo{cipher}w_whisper.rs
   524â†’    vo{cipher}mc_*.md    # CLAUDE.md templates
   525â†’    {ACRONYM}-*.adoc     # Concept models
   526â†’----
   527â†’+
   528â†’**VVK Special Status:**
   529â†’VVK (Voce Viva Kit) is a
   530â†’{vose_kit}
   531â†’with unique characteristics:
   532â†’+
   533â†’* Has a
   534â†’{vose_whisper}
   535â†’like other kits (for CLAUDE.md knowledge)
   536â†’* Contents are primarily binaries (`vvx-{platform}`) and the uninstall script
   537â†’* Installed like any other kit â€” `kits/vvk/` in
   538â†’{vose_parcel}
   539â†’becomes `Tools/vvk/` in target
   540â†’* Present in every
   541â†’{vosp_target_repo}
   542â†’â€” the universal kit
   543â†’* Uses cipher `vv` (Voce Viva)
   544â†’
   545â†’[[vose_managed_section]]
   546â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_entityâź§
   547â†’{vose_managed_section}::
   548â†’A block of content in the target CLAUDE.md file bounded by
   549â†’{vose_marker_s}.
   550â†’Each
   551â†’{vose_kit}
   552â†’may contribute multiple
   553â†’{vose_managed_section_s}
   554â†’for distinct feature groups.
   555â†’+
   556â†’Members:
   557â†’
   558â†’[[vosem_file]]
   559â†’// âź¦axl_voices axr_member axd_requiredâź§
   560â†’* {vosem_file} - Template file path relative to
   561â†’{vosp_veiled}
   562â†’(e.g., `vocjjmc_core.md`)
   563â†’
   564â†’[[vosem_tag]]
   565â†’// âź¦axl_voices axr_member axd_requiredâź§
   566â†’* {vosem_tag} - Identifier used in
   567â†’{vose_marker_s}
   568â†’(e.g., `JJK`, `JJK_GALLOPS`)
   569â†’
   570â†’[[vose_marker]]
   571â†’// âź¦axl_voices axt_stringâź§
   572â†’{vose_marker}::
   573â†’Delimiters bounding a
   574â†’{vose_managed_section}
   575â†’in CLAUDE.md.
   576â†’+
   577â†’Format: `<\!-- MANAGED:{tag}:BEGIN -->` and `<\!-- MANAGED:{tag}:END -->`
   578â†’+
   579â†’Content between
   580â†’{vose_marker_s}
   581â†’is replaced entirely on
   582â†’{vosoi_install}.
   583â†’Content outside
   584â†’{vose_marker_s}
   585â†’is preserved.
   586â†’
   587â†’[[vose_uninstalled_marker]]
   588â†’// âź¦axl_voices axt_stringâź§
   589â†’{vose_uninstalled_marker}::
   590â†’Placeholder preserving position of a removed
   591â†’{vose_managed_section}.

   818â†’[[vosor_release]]
   819â†’// âź¦axl_voices axo_command axe_bash_scripted axd_transientâź§
   820â†’=== {vosor_release}
   821â†’
   822â†’Creates a
   823â†’{vose_parcel}
   824â†’from the
   825â†’{vosp_kit_forge}.

   827â†’{voss_inputs}
   828â†’
   829â†’// âź¦axd_noneâź§
   830â†’No external inputs. Operates on current
   831â†’{vosp_kit_forge}
   832â†’state.
   833â†’
   834â†’{voss_behavior}
   835â†’
   836â†’. *Collect kit declarations*:

   843â†’
   844â†’. *Validate prefix compliance*:

   846â†’{vose_kit}:
   847â†’{vosc_require}
   848â†’all asset
   849â†’{vosls_signet_s}
   850â†’derive from
   851â†’{voslc_cipher}

   854â†’{vosli_inscription_s}
   855â†’
   856â†’. *Run testbenches*:
   857â†’.. {vosc_call}
   858â†’each kit's testbench
   859â†’.. {vosc_fatal}
   860â†’if any tests fail
   861â†’
   862â†’. *Build binaries*:
   863â†’.. {vosc_call}
   864â†’cargo build for each target platform
   865â†’.. {vosc_store}
   866â†’binary paths
   867â†’
   868â†’. *Collect assets*:
   869â†’.. For each
   870â†’{vose_kit}:
   871â†’collect files excluding
   872â†’{vosp_veiled}
   873â†’.. For each
   874â†’{voslc_cipher}
   875â†’from kit list:
   876â†’... Scan kit forge `.claude/commands/` for files matching `{cipher}c-*.md`
   877â†’... Scan kit forge `.claude/hooks/` for files matching `{cipher}h-*.md`
   878â†’... Copy matching files to parcel `claude/commands/` and `claude/hooks/` respectively
   879â†’.. {vosc_step}
   880â†’"Claude config assets collected by cipher pattern"
   881â†’
   882â†’. *Generate sigil*:
   883â†’.. {vosc_store}
   884â†’{vost_sigil}
   885â†’as current timestamp (YYMMDD-HHMM)
   886â†’
   887â†’. *Compute brand identity*:
   888â†’.. {vosc_call}
   889â†’compute order-independent super-SHA:
   890â†’... Sort all parcel files by path
   891â†’... Hash each file's path + content
   892â†’... Combine hashes
   893â†’... Exclude
   894â†’{vose_brand_file}
   895â†’from computation
   896â†’.. {vosc_store}
   897â†’super-SHA for registry lookup
   898â†’
   899â†’. *Allocate hallmark*:
   900â†’.. {vosc_call}
   901â†’read
   902â†’{vose_registry}
   903â†’.. If super-SHA exists in registry:
   904â†’{vosc_store}
   905â†’existing
   906â†’{vost_hallmark}
   907â†’.. If super-SHA is new:
   908â†’... {vosc_store}
   909â†’next sequential
   910â†’{vost_hallmark}
   911â†’(max + 1, starting at 1000)
   912â†’... {vosc_call}
   913â†’update
   914â†’{vose_registry}
   915â†’with new entry
   916â†’... {vosc_call}
   917â†’commit registry change
   918â†’
   919â†’. *Write brand file*:
   920â†’.. {vosc_call}
   921â†’create
   922â†’{vose_brand_file}
   923â†’at parcel root with
   924â†’{vosem_hallmark},
   925â†’{vosem_brand_date},
   926â†’{vosem_brand_sha},
   927â†’{vosem_brand_commit}
   928â†’(current git HEAD),
   929â†’{vosem_brand_kits}
   930â†’(list of kit identifiers)
   931â†’
   932â†’. *Package parcel*:
   933â†’.. Create `vvk-parcel-{hallmark}.tar.gz` with structure:
   934â†’... `vvi_install.sh` â€” install script (bootstrap, not installed)
   935â†’... `vvbf_brand.json` â€”
   936â†’{vose_brand_file}
   937â†’at parcel root
   938â†’... `kits/` â€” all kit assets by directory (including VVK with binaries)
   939â†’... `claude/` â€” commands and hooks collected by cipher pattern
   940â†’
   941â†’{voss_outputs}
   942â†’
   943â†’* {vose_parcel}
   944â†’archive: `vvk-parcel-{hallmark}.tar.gz`
   945â†’* {vose_brand_file}
   946â†’at parcel root
   947â†’
   948â†’{voss_completion}

   960â†’Deploys a
   961â†’{vose_parcel}

   963â†’{vosp_target_repo}.

  1002â†’
  1003â†’. *Validate git state*:

  1013â†’. *Read brand identity*:

  1016â†’{vose_brand_file}

  1018â†’.. {vosc_store}
  1019â†’{vosem_hallmark}
  1020â†’and
  1021â†’{vosem_brand_kits}
  1022â†’
  1023â†’. *Validate kit match*:

  1025â†’`BURC_MANAGED_KITS` exactly matches
  1026â†’{vosem_brand_kits}

  1030â†’
  1031â†’. *Nuclear cleanup*:

  1033â†’{vosc_call}

  1035â†’.. If `.vvk/` absent: no action (first-time install)
  1036â†’.. For each kit in `BURC_MANAGED_KITS`:

  1038â†’{vosc_call}

  1041â†’.. {vosc_step}

  1043â†’
  1044â†’. *Create brand directory*:
  1045â†’.. {vosc_call}

  1049â†’{vose_brand_file}
  1050â†’to `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json`
  1051â†’
  1052â†’. *Install kits*:

  1106â†’{voss_completion}
